channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Marvel Rivals Aims to Become ‘Moving Anime Experience’
item.content:encoded:
Marvel Rivals wants to be so much more than a video game hero shooter. The free-to-play game aims to be an extension of the Marvel Cinematic Universe and a comprehensive moving anime.

The game’s creative director, Guangyun Chen, recently discussed what the team’s goals are through 2026 and beyond.

Marvel Rivals: A Moving Anime

Speaking to FRVR, Chen said the team wants to push boundaries in 2026. To do that, the focus will shift. The game is going to expand and become significantly more PvE-focused with more tie-ins to the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

“We are moving beyond standard 6v6 PvP,” Chen explained. “This includes the expansion into new PvE modes and Path to Doomsday. The event will feature five MCU-related updates inspired by the Infinity Saga, including new game modes and themed content.”

Furthermore, Chen states that the ultimate goal is to become a moving anime experience. Path to Doomsday is said to be a primary thematic driver for the rest of the year in the free-to-play title. More details on future updates to come later.

“With over 9,000 Marvel characters to draw from, we aren’t worried about running out of options soon,” said Chen, noting that the current plan maps out content through 2027.

Insider Gaming will continue to report any and all updates on Marvel Rivals. For more on the game, check out the details on the Lower Manhattan update.

How do you feel about these plans for the hero shooter? Do you like the idea of MCU tie-ins? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Naughty Dog Working On New Uncharted Game, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A recent claim indicates that the Uncharted franchise may be making a comeback. The series has been a staple of PlayStation for nearly two decades, with the last release being the remastered Uncharted: Legacy of Thieves Collection, featuring Uncharted 4 and its expansion, Uncharted: The Lost Legacy.

A Thief’s End was the last mainline release for the franchise. It’s been nearly a decade since 2017, when that game was released, and fans do want a new experience.

Naughty Dog’s Secret Game May Be a New Uncharted

In 2025, Naughty Dog studio head Neil Druckmann revealed that the studio was working on another game apart from Intergalactic. However, he claims to be in a producer and mentor role for the unannounced title.

Now, a new social media claim from @AlexandreNGamR states that Naughty Dog is developing a new Uncharted alongside “Intergalactic.” This claim has not been confirmed by Insider Gaming as of yet.

Uncharted: The Lost Legacy’s director, Shaun Escayg, recently may have teased a new project for the franchise. He’d posted an image on social media of a cannon overlooking a beachfront city from a cliff. The caption claimed it was all for “research.” Learn more at this link.

More on the Future of PlayStation

Reportedly, there will be a Sony PlayStation State of Play on April 16. The last State of Play in February announced several titles, including the shadowdropped God of War: Sons of Sparta. SAROS was also featured. Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls, 007 First Light, and the return of Castlevania are slated for a 2026 release.

While it’s unknown if an Uncharted title is truly in the works, all the evidence points to a positive outcome for fans of the franchise.

Insider Gaming will continue to report on the latest gaming news.

What are you hoping to see from a new Uncharted? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Gameplay of League of Legends’ New Champion Locke Leaks
item.content:encoded:
Locke, the next champion set to release in League of Legends, has allegedly had footage of his gameplay leak online.

Locke was confirmed as LoL’s next champion unintentionally by a Rioter in an April Fools’ tweet.

The Demacian character is expected to launch sometime in Season 2, 2026, which will take place between April 29 and August 12.

So far, the champion’s only appearance was in a motion comic released by Riot earlier in the year. Though his in-game appearance appears to be very different to the cloaked figure we were shown previously.

Well-known LoL leaker ‘Big Bad Bear’ has released a video which gives an alleged first look at the upcoming champion.

Before this video, there had been rumours that Locke will be an AP fighter, played in the jungle or mid lane, who throws around giant ghostly nails like Nobara from the Jujutsu Kaisen anime.

And that rumour appears to be correct, as the leaked footage shows Locke throwing around a giant nail in what is claimed to be his Q.

After that, the video also shows Locke going into a second, even more ghostly form, which appears to deal AoE damage in a circle around him.

Additionally, there are spooky “sealed artefacts” which spawn around the map for Locke to pick up, but what they do is not yet known.

This ghostly theme likely ties in with the piece of lore we were given about Locke in the motion comic.

Speaking at a tavern, Locke receives a warning from a bartender who says:

“No one that goes into those woods comes back, at least not the same as they were.”

It has been confirmed by Riot Games’ senior vice president Andrei ‘Meddler’ van Roon that only one new LoL champion will be released in 2026.

This is so that the publishers focus on developing ‘LoL Next’, a large-scale revamp for the MOBA.

However, Meddler has also confirmed that one other Demacian champion is receiving a rework following on from the Shyvana VGU.

Meddler said work on the VGU was “in flight” which has the community speculating that it’s for Quinn or Nocturne.

With leaks confirming a demon theme for LoL season 2, Nocturne might be the more likely option.

What LoL champion would you like to see receive a VGU? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Epic Games to Release ARC Raiders-Style Disney Game This Year
item.content:encoded:
Epic Games is pinning a Fortnite resurgence on the Disney partnership. Recently, Epic laid off 1,000 employees and announced it would shut down several Fortnite game modes.

Now, a new report provides some insight into what’s next for the company, and the hope that partnering with the Walt Disney Company can provide a boost.

Epic Planning Disney-Themed ARC Raiders-Like Shooter

According to a new Bloomberg report, four current and former Epic employees say the company is on track to launch the first game under its new Disney partnership in November. The game is slated to be an Extraction Shooter like ARC Raiders. Disney characters are slated to battle enemies until they reach an extraction point.

However, there could be trouble on the horizon.

“So far, internal reviewers have expressed concerns that the game mechanics are not very original, but some of the employees are optimistic that Epic will get it right by the launch date,” the report says.

Epic Games Partnership Led to Increased Disney+ Subscriptions

Epic is reportedly banking on its comeback coming from games developed in partnership with Disney. Disney invested $1.5 billion in Epic two years ago. This Disney collaboration will produce at least two additional games.

“We remain focused on our long-term collaboration with Epic, which continues to have strong momentum, and our work to build a transformational games and entertainment universe remains unchanged,” said a Disney spokesperson.

As part of the agreement, content from Marvel, Star Wars, Avatar, and Pixar will be added to Fortnite. Collaborations between Fortnite and the Epic Games Store have reportedly contributed to an unexpected increase in Disney+ subscriptions.

Insider Gaming will continue to provide news on the upcoming shooter as more information becomes available.

What do you think about the news of this upcoming Extraction Shooter? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1757: Hints and Answer for April 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1757 for April 11, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, April 11, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1757 is an easier puzzle than the last few. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4 out of 6 guesses. That makes it a full guess harder than the 5.3-guess average from April 10.

General Wordle Hints For April 11

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It describes someone who is easily offended by certain types of behavior.
  • Hint 2: Often associated with being overly modest or conservative.
  • Hint 3: The term can sometimes be used in a slightly critical or teasing way.
  • Hint 4: It relates more to attitude and personality than actions

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1757 for April 11, 2026.

Starter Words For April 11, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • PRIDE
  • ERUPT
  • PURGE
  • DRUPE

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1757 On April 11, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: PRUDE

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

PRUDE is a noun that means “a person who is excessively proper or modest in speech, conduct, dress, etc.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1756 on April 10, 2026

Answer: CAROM

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1757?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on April 12 will be Wordle Game #1758.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
